title: hypericum quadrangulum subsp. corsicum
intro: Hypericum quadrangulum subsp. corsicum, also known as Corsican St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort genus, native to the western Mediterranean region. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial compounds that have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Corsican St. John's Wort include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote a healthy balance of bodily functions.
